L1.area: Gateway WebSocket connection and session initialization

L2.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists where certain authenticated WebSocket connections could self-declare elevated privilege scopes. A logic flaw in the scope validation path allowed shared-authentication connections to preserve client-supplied scopes that were not bound to a device identity or explicitly trusted connection type, enabling unauthorized access to administrative operations.

L3.area: Gateway WebSocket connection and session initialization
L3.description: An authorization bypass vulnerability exists where certain authenticated WebSocket connections could self-declare elevated privilege scopes. A logic flaw in the scope validation path allowed shared-authentication connections to preserve client-supplied scopes that were not bound to a device identity or explicitly trusted connection type, enabling unauthorized access to administrative operations.

ground_truth.reason: The gateway WebSocket connection handler did not clear client-declared scopes for shared-token and shared-password authenticated connections, allowing device-less operators to self-declare elevated scopes such as operator.admin and perform administrative operations.
